entries.verdict_line: S&P 500 at all-time highs while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — the most expensive soft landing in history, and India's FIIs just blinked.
Airline passengers down 9% to a decade-low 35 million while GST prints ₹2 lakh crore — India's consumption story is splitting along a fault line the headline numbers hide.
Copper at a 10-year high of $5.94 while US GDP prints 0.7% SAAR — global demand signals are contradicting each other, and India's CAD is caught in the crossfire.
Copper at an all-time high of $6.03 while India VIX jumps to 18.46 — industrial metals are pricing a boom that India's own volatility gauge refuses to believe.
Brent down 5.2% to $108 while SIP inflows slip to ₹29,845 cr — India's retail bid is softening precisely when the oil windfall should be fuelling it.
Nikkei at a decade-high 59,513 while US GDP prints 0.7% — global equities are pricing a recovery that the world's largest economy has not yet delivered.
Gold at $4,644 in USD but ₹2,000/gram at a 10-year floor — INR weakness should be lifting gold in rupee terms, yet it isn't; someone large is selling.
